Part B.key_evidence: Positive subtraction in r-band with magpsf ~18.84 and small uncertainty. Science shows a bright source with an apparent extended companion/host nearby; reference shows a galaxy-like structure at the same location. sgscore1=0.006 (PS1 nearest neighbor is galaxy-like) at distpsnr1=4.74 arcsec, nmtchps=9, with PS1 colors indicating an extended/host source. ndethist=18 over deltajd=18 days indicates repeated detections on a short baseline. Difference image shows a compact positive residual near the center.
Part B.leading_interpretation_and_support: Most consistent with a supernova: a new point-like positive residual offset from a galaxy-like host seen in the reference, low sgscore1 indicating an extended nearby PS1 source, and persistent detections over ~18 days consistent with an SN light curve timescale rather than a single-night asteroid.
Part B.alternative_analysis: An AGN is possible given the host association and repeated detections, but the short 18-day detection span and offset point source are more SN-like; a variable star is disfavored because the PS1 neighbor is galaxy-like (sgscore1 ~0).
